hero.lead: Multiple waypoints + altitude + drone view. Design free-form cinematic flights over PLATEAU 3D city models. Chain points, dial in altitude, pitch and damping — craft your own flight line.

section01.title: Compose in 3 steps
section01.sub: Place points, set the camera, hit Play. Just follow the order.
section01.step1Body: Tap the map or search a place name to drop 5–50 waypoints. No A→B routing constraint — sketch a free flight line that doesn't follow the road.
section01.step2Body: Edit each point's altitude (altM), pitch (pitchDeg), heading offset (headingRelDeg), hover seconds, and corner radius. Cruising speed is also tunable per segment.
section01.step3Body: The drone-view camera interpolates smoothly between your points. Tune the feel with τ damping and lookahead — after Stop, the mouse takes over so you can roam the city freely (see FREE LOOK below).

section02.title: Design any camera path you want
section02.sub: Not A→B routing — you compose the shot. Multi-point + per-point altitude + per-segment speed + AI prompt.
section02.feat1Body: Free flight unbound by roads. Sneak between buildings, orbit overhead, or trace a custom loop — points fire in the order you place them.
section02.feat2Body: 5 m above ground here, 80 m there. Dial in altitude, pitch, and heading offset for every point so the shot can rise and dip cinematically.
section02.feat3Body: Try a prompt like "From Skytree to the Imperial Palace — low → climb → orbit" and get the composition JSON instantly. No need to start from a blank canvas.

section02_5.title: More freedom in your camera path
section02_5.sub: Place search, multi-point + altitude, URL sharing, and a community board — every tool you need to play beyond a single A→B route.
section02_5.feat1Body: Type a landmark, address, or station — Google Places Autocomplete (Tokyo 23 wards only) returns suggestions you can drop into your camera path with a single tap. Works even if you can't type Japanese.
section02_5.feat2Body: Up to 50 waypoints with per-point altitude, pitch, and heading offset. Break free from A→B routing constraints — craft a flight line that's truly yours.
section02_5.feat3Body: Every point + per-point parameter + global setting packed into a single URL. The receiver opens the link, presses Play, and gets your exact shot. JSON export + import also supported.
section02_5.feat4Body: The COMMUNITY section below lists user-shared compositions. Anonymous-friendly, no signup. Tap any card to launch it right back into this app.

section03.title: Tune the cinematic feel
section03.sub: Beyond per-point altitude and pitch, four global dials shape the overall feel. If the camera feels mechanical or swings hard on corners, nudge one value and try again.
section03.altBody: Eye height above ground. Low gives a street-level walk; high gives an aerial overview.
section03.pitchBody: Camera depression angle. Negative looks down; positive looks up. Around -10° is the standard cruising attitude.
section03.lookaheadBody: How far ahead (in meters) the drone looks to determine heading. Too small — jittery on tight bends; larger — smoother and more gradual.
section03.tauBody: Shared time constant for heading response and route-line smoothing. Smaller = snappier and straighter; larger = smoother and more curved. 0 disables both.
section03.tip: Start with Lookahead 50 m, Damping 0.5 s. To turn earlier, increase Lookahead. For a snappier response, lower Damping.

section04.title: After Stop — explore the city on your own
section04.sub: The moment auto-flight ends or you hit Stop, camera control returns to your mouse. Lock is released — orbit the path or any building, compose any angle.
section04.orbitBody: Left-click and drag to orbit the camera around the path and buildings. Sweep directions; circle the entire cityscape.
section04.zoomBody: Scroll to move the camera forward or back — zoom in on a single building or pull out for a bird's-eye view. Right-drag up/down works the same way.
section04.tiltBody: Hold Ctrl and right-drag to tilt the pitch angle. Drag up to look skyward; drag down for a straight-down overhead view. Look up at a building from below, lock in a 30° composition, or craft your own drone-photo angle. This is the most fun part.
section04.tip: Press Play again to return to auto-flight with the same settings. The cycle — fly → Stop → compose your angle → Play again — transforms the same path every time.

section05.title: Built with
section05.sub: Python backend, WebGL frontend, Cloud Run infrastructure. Keyless CI/CD deployment.

crossLink.heading: Other missions in the trilogy

route.title: TOKYO 3D Route
route.desc: Two-point route flythrough in drone view

crossLink.body: Want to inspect individual buildings instead of flying a route? Plateau 3D App has you covered. Every building in Tokyo's 23 wards rendered in LOD2 — click any one to read its attributes.

viewer.title: tokyo-3d-drone
viewer.resetTitle: Clear A/B and route, return to Tokyo Station
viewer.clearAbTitle: Clear A/B, waypoints, and route (keep map view)
viewer.waypointHint: After A/B is set, click the map to add waypoints (max 8, within 1.5 km of both A and B)
viewer.lodHint: Changes take effect on the next Fetch Route → Play.
viewer.errorAbBothRequired: Please set both Point A and Point B
viewer.camLookaheadTitle: How far ahead the drone looks to decide heading. Larger values smooth tight bends.
viewer.camTauTitle: Time constant for heading response. Larger = smoother and more gradual; smaller = snappier.

search.hint: Search by place name, address, or landmark (Tokyo 23 wards only)
search.outOfRange: This query is outside the Tokyo 23 wards coverage. Please try a place inside the 23 wards.
search.noMatch: No matching place found. Try a different keyword.
search.errorAutocomplete: Search failed. Please try again in a moment.
search.errorOutOfRange: This place is outside the Tokyo 23 wards coverage.
search.errorAbTooFar: Too far from A. Please pick a place within 1.5 km.
search.errorWpTooFar: Too far from A or B. Place must be within 1.5 km of both.

creator.applyAllParams: → Apply ALL params to every point

creator.applyAllSegmentParamsHint: Copy duration / speed from this segment to every other segment
